title: Addition of Sinusoids
summary: This page explains the phase relationship between sine and cosine functions, showing that sin lags cos by 90 degrees. It then derives how two sinusoids of the same frequency but different phases combine into a single sinusoid, expressing the resultant amplitude C and phase θ in terms of the original coefficients. The phasor representation is introduced to geometrically illustrate this addition.

figures.caption: Graphical representation of a complex number C = a - jb in the complex plane, showing its magnitude C and angle θ below the real axis.
figures.description: The figure shows the complex plane with the horizontal real axis labeled Re and the vertical imaginary axis labeled Im. A point representing the complex number C = a - jb lies in the fourth quadrant at coordinates (a, -b), with dashed projections to the axes marking the real part a and imaginary part -b. A vector from the origin to the point is labeled C, and the angle θ is drawn below the real axis. Students should notice that the positive real part and negative imaginary part place the number below the real axis, and that the vector’s length is the magnitude of the complex number.
